artifacts.title: Good receipt from 2026-06-07
artifacts.excerpt:
My wedding advice:

1) invite those who will always be there for you through thick and thin, in life and death (eg. eternal relations)
2) make your marriage vows in front of them, and ask them to help you keep those vows.
3) dress in a way that shows you are in love, and ask everyone else to dress well and gaily
4) hire a good photographer
5) remember this day is for you to look your spouse in the eyes and to promise her that you will be one with her, in life, in death, in legacy, in name, in family, in wealth, in health, in sickness, in home, in public. It's for you two to make a public union, whatever union you most want. Just remember, today is when you get the power of your whole community to help you make a union real.

Everything else is optional, imo. You can't really do the above steps for less than $1000 easily, but you can definitely do it for far less than $10,000. Our wedding
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: good, want, really, don.

men are delaying fatherhood to boyboss

imo, (biased because I'm a man, I guess) men intentionally postponing family formation is a much larger effect than women postponing family formation.

artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: want, help, should, friends.

artifacts.title: Want receipt from 2025-12-04
artifacts.excerpt:
Wait, I'm realizing that the desire to retvrn isn't actually a desire to return to a better America, but a desire to return to a better advertising environment.

Our life is intermediated via ads and news and social media. It's literally the medium we breathe. The 60s weren't better, but their ads were more optimistic, beautiful, depicting a beautiful life.

What people actually want is to live in a culture that reveres beauty, family life, tradition, a nice home, and the American dream.

They are sick of corporatism, leftism, negativity, political fighting.

They desperately want a new propaganda.

The post war and cold war American propaganda machine was so powerful that it created the illusion of a perfect country. A perfect 60s, one that obviously never existed on Earth, but which DOES exist here, now, in the noosphere, in our memory, and in the cultural artifacts we dig up when we t
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: want, help, should, friends.

artifacts.title: Want receipt from 2022-03-02
artifacts.excerpt:
TIL: during the golden age of Athens, the city was about 0.7 sq miles, and held 75,000 people.

For perspective (pop/sq mile):
- (Ancient) Athens: 107,000
- Manhattan: 69,468
- LA: 8,499
- SF: 6,266

If we want a golden age, we should consider raising the density. A lot.
